title: Merkle Tree for Data Integrity
instruction: Implement a MerkleTree class in Python. Constructor takes a list of strings (leaves). Expose: root_hash -> str (hex digest), get_proof(index: int) -> list[dict] where each dict has 'hash' and 'direction' ('left'|'right'), verify_proof(leaf: str, proof: list[dict], root: str) -> bool. Use SHA-256. Handle odd number of leaves by duplicating the last one. No external dependencies.

test_commands:
python3 -c "
from solution_0 import MerkleTree
import hashlib

# deterministic root
leaves = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd']
tree = MerkleTree(leaves)
assert len(tree.root_hash) == 64  # sha256 hex
assert tree.root_hash == MerkleTree(leaves).root_hash  # deterministic
print('deterministic root: PASS')

# proof and verify
for i in range(len(leaves)):
    proof = tree.get_proof(i)
    assert tree.verify_proof(leaves[i], proof, tree.root_hash), f'proof failed for index {i}'
print('proof verify: PASS')

# tampered leaf fails
proof0 = tree.get_proof(0)
assert not tree.verify_proof('tampered', proof0, tree.root_hash)
print('tamper detection: PASS')

# odd number of leaves
tree_odd = MerkleTree(['x', 'y', 'z'])
assert len(tree_odd.root_hash) == 64
proof_z = tree_odd.get_proof(2)
assert tree_odd.verify_proof('z', proof_z, tree_odd.root_hash)
print('odd leaves: PASS')

print('ALL PASS')
"
